title: The half hitch (right to left)
subtitle: Mirror direction for diagonal bars that run from right to left
excerpt: The right-to-left half hitch is the mirror of the left-to-right version. Two consecutive right-to-left half hitches form a right-to-left double half hitch, used to build diagonal bars that slope upward from left to right. Knowing both directions gives full control over diagonal bar angles in macramé patterns.
sourceNotes: The half hitch is a standard knot in rope work and macramé. Observation-led.

content.text: is the mirror of the left-to-right half hitch. It produces

that rise from right to left, and spiral columns that twist in the opposite direction from the left-to-right version. Most macramé patterns use both directions to create V shapes, X shapes, and chevron rows.
Pin one cord horizontally as the carrier cord. The working cord begins on the right side.

steps: Hold the working cord in your right hand. Bring it over the carrier cord from right to left.
Loop the working cord under the carrier cord, moving left to right.
Bring the working cord up and through the loop it has formed on the right side of itself.
Pull the working cord downward to tighten. One right-to-left half hitch is seated on the carrier.
As with the left-to-right half hitch, a single half hitch does not lock. Follow with a second right-to-left half hitch to form the stable double half hitch.

content.text: Chevron rows use two carrier cords: one angled toward the lower left (knotted with left-to-right half hitches working outward from centre) and one angled toward the lower right (knotted with right-to-left half hitches working outward). V rows and X rows are built on the same principle, with the carrier cords held at contrasting angles to create the shapes.

attrs.intro: Two problems encountered when practising mirror-direction half hitches.

items.fix: Pin the carrier cord at both ends before beginning the row. Keep the working cord tension even across all knots.
items.cause: The carrier cord was not held at a consistent angle throughout the row.
items.symptom: Diagonal bar curves rather than holding a straight angle

items.fix: Widen the angle of the two carrier cords at the start of the V. The meeting point drops lower and the shape opens.
items.cause: Carrier cords pulled too steeply inward.
items.symptom: V shape closes up too tightly

attrs.heading: Common problems
